exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.description: A compound chest exercise performed lying on a flat bench, pressing a barbell from chest level to full arm extension.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with feet firmly on the ground
Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder width
Unrack the bar and hold it above your chest with arms extended
Lower the bar to your mid-chest in a controlled manner
Press the bar back up to the starting position
Keep your shoulder blades retracted throughout the movement

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.description: An upper chest exercise performed on an incline bench with dumbbells, emphasizing the clavicular head of the pectorals.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline
Sit back with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs
Kick the dumbbells up to shoulder level as you lean back
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended
Lower the dumbbells slowly to chest level with elbows at roughly 45 degrees
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions

exercises.name: Decline Barbell Press
exercises.description: A chest pressing movement performed on a decline bench, targeting the lower portion of the pectorals.
instructions: Secure your legs at the end of a decline bench and lie back
Grip the barbell at slightly wider than shoulder width
Unrack the bar and lower it to your lower chest
Press the bar back up to full arm extension
Keep your core braced throughout the movement

exercises.name: Push-ups
exercises.description: A classic bodyweight exercise that builds chest, shoulder, and tricep strength using your own body as resistance.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulder width
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows
Push back up to the starting position by extending your arms
Maintain a tight core throughout the movement

exercises.name: Cable Flyes
exercises.description: An isolation exercise for the chest performed using cable pulleys, providing constant tension throughout the range of motion.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to shoulder height
Grab one handle in each hand and step forward into a staggered stance
Start with arms extended out to the sides with a slight bend in the elbows
Bring your hands together in front of your chest in a hugging motion
Slowly return to the starting position under control

exercises.name: Dumbbell Flyes
exercises.description: A chest isolation exercise performed lying on a flat bench, opening the arms wide to stretch and contract the pectorals.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand above your chest
Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout the movement
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc
Lower until you feel a stretch in your chest
Bring the dumbbells back together above your chest in the same arc

exercises.name: Chest Dips
exercises.description: A compound bodyweight exercise performed on parallel bars with a forward lean to emphasize chest activation.
instructions: Grip the parallel bars and lift yourself to the starting position with arms extended
Lean your torso forward approximately 30 degrees
Lower your body by bending your elbows until you feel a stretch in your chest
Push yourself back up to the starting position
Keep your elbows flared slightly outward to target the chest

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.description: A fundamental compound exercise that targets the entire posterior chain by lifting a loaded barbell from the floor to hip level.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart with the barbell over mid-foot
Bend at the hips and knees to grip the bar just outside your legs
Keep your chest up and back flat as you brace your core
Drive through your heels and extend your hips and knees to lift the bar
Stand tall at the top with shoulders back and hips fully extended
Lower the bar back to the floor in a controlled manner

exercises.name: Pull-ups
exercises.description: A compound upper body pulling exercise performed by hanging from a bar and pulling yourself up until your chin clears the bar.
instructions: Grip the pull-up bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder width, palms facing away
Hang with arms fully extended and shoulders engaged
Pull yourself upward by driving your elbows down toward your hips
Continue until your chin is above the bar
Lower yourself back to the starting position under control

exercises.name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
exercises.description: A compound back exercise performed by rowing a barbell toward the torso while hinged at the hips, building lat and mid-back thickness.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a barbell with an overhand grip
Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to the floor
Let the bar hang at arm's length below your shoulders
Pull the bar toward your lower chest by driving your elbows back
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top
Lower the bar back to the starting position under control

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.description: A machine-based pulling exercise that mimics the pull-up motion, targeting the latissimus dorsi muscles.
instructions: Sit at the lat pulldown machine and adjust the thigh pad for a snug fit
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width with palms facing away
Pull the bar down toward your upper chest while leaning slightly back
Squeeze your lats at the bottom of the movement
Slowly return the bar to the starting position with arms extended

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single-Arm Row
exercises.description: A unilateral back exercise performed with one arm at a time, allowing you to focus on each side independently.
instructions: Place one knee and the same-side hand on a flat bench for support
Hold a dumbbell in the opposite hand with arm extended
Pull the dumbbell toward your hip by driving your elbow back and up
Squeeze your lat at the top of the movement
Lower the dumbbell back to full arm extension
Complete all reps on one side before switching

exercises.name: Cable Seated Row
exercises.description: A back exercise performed on a cable row station, building mid-back thickness and lat width with constant cable tension.
instructions: Sit at the cable row station with feet on the foot plates and knees slightly bent
Grab the handle with both hands and sit upright with arms extended
Pull the handle toward your lower chest by retracting your shoulder blades
Squeeze your back muscles at the peak contraction
Slowly extend your arms back to the starting position

exercises.name: T-Bar Row
exercises.description: A compound rowing movement using a landmine attachment or T-bar row machine, effectively building back thickness.
instructions: Straddle the T-bar with feet shoulder-width apart
Hinge at the hips and grip the handles with both hands
Keep your back flat and chest up
Pull the weight toward your chest by driving your elbows back
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top
Lower the weight back down under control

exercises.name: Chin-ups
exercises.description: A bodyweight pulling exercise similar to pull-ups but with an underhand grip, placing greater emphasis on the biceps.
instructions: Grip the pull-up bar with hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing toward you
Hang with arms fully extended and shoulders engaged
Pull yourself upward by driving your elbows down
Continue until your chin is above the bar
Lower yourself back to the starting position under control

exercises.name: Barbell Overhead Press
exercises.description: A compound shoulder exercise performed by pressing a barbell from shoulder level to overhead, building pressing strength and shoulder mass.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a barbell at shoulder height
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width with palms facing forward
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes
Press the bar overhead until your arms are fully extended
Move your head slightly forward once the bar clears it
Lower the bar back to shoulder height under control

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.description: A seated or standing pressing movement with dumbbells that develops all three heads of the deltoids.
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support or stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward
Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended
Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height under control
Avoid arching your lower back excessively

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
exercises.description: An isolation exercise that targets the lateral head of the deltoid, creating shoulder width and the capped shoulder look.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides
Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout the movement
Raise both dumbbells out to the sides until your arms are parallel to the floor
Hold briefly at the top, then lower the dumbbells back to your sides
Avoid swinging or using momentum to lift the weights

exercises.name: Dumbbell Front Raise
exercises.description: An isolation exercise targeting the anterior deltoid by raising dumbbells to the front of the body.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs
Keep a slight bend in your elbows
Raise one or both dumbbells in front of you to shoulder height
Hold briefly at the top, then lower under control
Avoid swinging the weights or leaning back

exercises.name: Face Pulls
exercises.description: A cable exercise that targets the rear deltoids and upper back, improving posture and shoulder health.
instructions: Set a cable pulley to upper chest or face height with a rope attachment
Grip the rope with both hands using an overhand grip
Step back to create tension in the cable
Pull the rope toward your face, separating your hands as you pull
Squeeze your rear deltoids and upper back at the peak contraction
Return to the starting position under control

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Fly
exercises.description: An isolation exercise for the rear deltoids performed bent over, strengthening the posterior shoulder and upper back.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at the hips with a flat back
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms hanging below your chest
Keep a slight bend in your elbows
Raise the dumbbells out to the sides by squeezing your rear deltoids
Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position under control

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.description: A classic bicep exercise performed by curling a barbell from hip level to shoulder level, building bicep size and strength.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a barbell with an underhand grip
Keep your elbows pinned to your sides
Curl the barbell up toward your shoulders by contracting your biceps
Squeeze at the top of the movement
Lower the barbell back to the starting position under control

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl
exercises.description: A unilateral or bilateral bicep exercise using dumbbells, allowing for supination and natural arm movement.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides
Keep your elbows close to your body
Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders, rotating your palms upward
Squeeze your biceps at the top
Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position under control

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
exercises.description: A bicep curl variation performed with a neutral grip that also targets the brachialis and brachioradialis muscles.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing each other
Keep your elbows pinned to your sides
Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders while maintaining the neutral grip
Squeeze at the top of the movement
Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position under control

exercises.name: Preacher Curl
exercises.description: An isolation bicep exercise performed on a preacher bench that eliminates momentum and emphasizes the bicep peak.
instructions: Sit at a preacher curl bench with your upper arms resting on the pad
Grip the EZ curl bar with an underhand grip
Start with your arms fully extended on the pad
Curl the bar upward by contracting your biceps
Squeeze at the top, then lower the bar back to full extension
Avoid letting your elbows lift off the pad

exercises.name: Cable Curl
exercises.description: A bicep curl performed using a cable machine, providing constant tension throughout the entire range of motion.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with the pulley set to the lowest position
Grip the straight bar or EZ bar attachment with an underhand grip
Keep your elbows pinned to your sides
Curl the bar upward by contracting your biceps
Squeeze at the top, then lower the bar back under control

exercises.name: Cable Tricep Pushdown
exercises.description: A tricep isolation exercise performed at a cable station, pushing a handle downward to fully extend the arms.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with the pulley set to the highest position
Grip the bar or rope attachment with an overhand grip
Keep your elbows pinned to your sides and upper arms stationary
Push the handle downward until your arms are fully extended
Squeeze your triceps at the bottom
Slowly return to the starting position

exercises.name: Skull Crushers
exercises.description: A tricep isolation exercise performed lying on a bench, lowering a barbell or EZ bar to the forehead and extending it back up.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding an EZ curl bar with a narrow overhand grip
Start with arms extended directly above your chest
Keep your upper arms stationary and bend at the elbows
Lower the bar toward your forehead in a controlled manner
Extend your arms back to the starting position by contracting your triceps

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.description: A tricep exercise performed by extending a dumbbell overhead, targeting the long head of the triceps.
instructions: Stand or sit holding a dumbbell with both hands behind your head
Keep your upper arms close to your ears and elbows pointing forward
Extend your arms upward until the dumbbell is overhead
Squeeze your triceps at the top
Lower the dumbbell back behind your head under control

exercises.name: Tricep Dips
exercises.description: A compound bodyweight exercise on parallel bars with an upright torso to emphasize the triceps.
instructions: Grip the parallel bars and lift yourself to the starting position with arms extended
Keep your torso upright and elbows close to your body
Lower your body by bending your elbows until they reach roughly 90 degrees
Push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your arms
Avoid flaring your elbows out to the sides

exercises.name: Close-Grip Bench Press
exercises.description: A bench press variation with a narrower grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench and grip the barbell with hands shoulder-width apart or slightly narrower
Unrack the bar and hold it above your chest with arms extended
Lower the bar to your mid-chest keeping your elbows close to your body
Press the bar back up to full extension by contracting your triceps
Keep your wrists straight throughout the movement

exercises.name: Wrist Curls
exercises.description: A forearm exercise performed by curling the wrist upward while holding a dumbbell, targeting the wrist flexors.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your forearms resting on your thighs, wrists hanging over your knees
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing up
Let the dumbbells roll down to your fingertips
Curl the dumbbells back up by flexing your wrists
Squeeze at the top and lower under control

exercises.name: Reverse Wrist Curls
exercises.description: A forearm exercise targeting the wrist extensors by curling the wrists upward with an overhand grip.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your forearms resting on your thighs, wrists hanging over your knees
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing down
Lower the dumbbells by flexing your wrists downward
Curl the dumbbells back up by extending your wrists
Squeeze at the top and lower under control

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.description: A fundamental compound lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps, glutes, and hamstrings by squatting down with a barbell across the upper back.
instructions: Position the barbell on your upper traps and rear delts in a squat rack
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed out
Take a deep breath and brace your core
Descend by breaking at the hips and knees simultaneously
Lower until thighs are at least parallel to the ground
Drive through your heels to stand back up

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.description: A squat variation with the barbell resting on the front of the shoulders, placing greater emphasis on the quadriceps and requiring more core stability.
instructions: Position the barbell across the front of your shoulders in a clean grip or cross-arm grip
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Keep your elbows high and chest up throughout the movement
Descend into a deep squat while keeping your torso as upright as possible
Drive through your heels to stand back up

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.description: A machine-based compound lower body exercise that allows you to push heavy loads safely while targeting the quadriceps and glutes.
instructions: Sit in the leg press machine with your back flat against the pad
Place your feet shoulder-width apart on the foot platform
Release the safety handles and lower the platform toward your chest
Descend until your knees are at approximately 90 degrees
Push the platform back up by extending your legs without locking your knees

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.description: An isolation exercise for the quadriceps performed on a machine by extending the legs from a bent to straight position.
instructions: Sit on the leg extension machine with your back against the pad
Adjust the pad so it rests on your lower shins just above the ankles
Grip the handles on the sides of the seat
Extend your legs until they are straight without locking your knees
Squeeze your quadriceps at the top, then lower under control

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.description: A single-leg squat variation with the rear foot elevated on a bench, challenging balance, stability, and unilateral leg strength.
instructions: Stand about two feet in front of a flat bench facing away from it
Place the top of one foot on the bench behind you
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides
Lower your body by bending your front knee until your thigh is parallel to the floor
Drive through your front heel to return to the starting position
Complete all reps on one side before switching

exercises.name: Walking Lunges
exercises.description: A dynamic lower body exercise performed by stepping forward into alternating lunges while traveling across the floor.
instructions: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand at your sides
Step forward with one leg and lower your body until both knees are at 90 degrees
Keep your front knee tracking over your toes without passing them
Push off your front foot and step forward with the opposite leg
Continue alternating legs as you walk forward

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.description: A hip-hinge exercise focusing on the hamstrings and lower back by lowering a barbell along the legs while keeping them nearly straight.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a barbell in front of your thighs
Keep a slight bend in your knees throughout the movement
Hinge at the hips and push them back as you lower the bar along your legs
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings
Drive your hips forward to return to the starting position
Keep your back flat and chest up throughout

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.description: An isolation exercise for the hamstrings performed face-down on a leg curl machine by curling the weight toward the glutes.
instructions: Lie face down on the leg curl machine with the pad just above your heels
Grip the handles on the sides of the bench
Curl your legs upward by contracting your hamstrings
Squeeze at the top when the pad is near your glutes
Lower the weight back to the starting position under control

exercises.name: Good Mornings
exercises.description: A barbell hip-hinge exercise that strengthens the hamstrings and lower back by bowing forward while keeping the legs relatively straight.
instructions: Position a barbell across your upper traps as you would for a squat
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees
Hinge at the hips and lean your torso forward while keeping your back flat
Lower until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor
Drive your hips forward to return to the upright position

exercises.name: Dumbbell Stiff-Leg Deadlift
exercises.description: A hamstring-focused deadlift variation using dumbbells with minimal knee bend, emphasizing the stretch and contraction of the hamstrings.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs
Keep your legs nearly straight with only a slight knee bend
Hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells along your legs
Lower until you feel a strong stretch in your hamstrings
Drive your hips forward to return to standing

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.description: A glute-focused exercise performed by driving the hips upward against a loaded barbell while the upper back is supported on a bench.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back resting against a flat bench
Roll a loaded barbell over your legs until it sits on your hip crease
Plant your feet flat on the floor about shoulder-width apart
Drive your hips upward by squeezing your glutes until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees
Hold the top position briefly, then lower your hips back down under control

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.description: A bodyweight glute activation exercise performed lying on the floor by driving the hips upward toward the ceiling.
instructions: Lie face up on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground
Keep your arms at your sides with palms facing down
Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes to lift your hips off the floor
Raise your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees
Hold at the top briefly, then lower back down under control

exercises.name: Cable Pull-Through
exercises.description: A hip-hinge exercise performed facing away from a cable machine, driving the hips forward to extend and target the glutes.
instructions: Set a cable pulley to the lowest position with a rope attachment
Straddle the cable facing away from the machine and grab the rope between your legs
Walk forward a few steps to create tension
Hinge at the hips and let the cable pull your hands back between your legs
Drive your hips forward and squeeze your glutes to stand tall

exercises.name: Kettlebell Swing
exercises.description: An explosive hip-hinge movement using a kettlebell that develops power in the glutes and hamstrings while improving cardiovascular conditioning.
instructions: Stand with feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart with a kettlebell on the floor in front of you
Hinge at the hips and grip the kettlebell with both hands
Hike the kettlebell back between your legs
Explosively drive your hips forward to swing the kettlebell up to chest height
Let the kettlebell swing back between your legs and repeat

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.description: An isolation exercise for the calves performed by raising onto the balls of the feet while standing, targeting the gastrocnemius muscle.
instructions: Stand on a raised platform or step with the balls of your feet on the edge
Let your heels drop below the platform to get a full stretch
Hold a dumbbell in one hand or use bodyweight for resistance
Raise up onto your toes as high as possible by contracting your calves
Hold briefly at the top, then lower your heels back down under control

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.description: A calf exercise performed in a seated position that specifically targets the soleus muscle of the lower leg.
instructions: Sit on a seated calf raise machine with the pad on your lower thighs
Place the balls of your feet on the foot platform
Release the safety lever
Lower your heels as far as possible for a full stretch
Push up through the balls of your feet and squeeze your calves at the top

exercises.name: Barbell Shrugs
exercises.description: A trap isolation exercise performed by shrugging the shoulders upward while holding a barbell, building upper trap mass.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip
Keep your arms straight throughout the movement
Elevate your shoulders straight up toward your ears
Squeeze your traps at the top and hold briefly
Lower your shoulders back to the starting position under control

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrugs
exercises.description: A trap exercise using dumbbells, allowing a greater range of motion than barbell shrugs and more natural shoulder movement.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides
Keep your arms straight and your chest up
Shrug your shoulders upward toward your ears
Squeeze your traps at the top and hold briefly
Lower your shoulders back down under control

exercises.name: Farmer's Walk
exercises.description: A loaded carry exercise that builds grip strength, trap size, and total-body stability by walking while holding heavy weights.
instructions: Stand between two heavy dumbbells or kettlebells
Bend down and grip one in each hand
Stand tall with shoulders back and core braced
Walk forward in a straight line with controlled, even steps
Maintain an upright posture and avoid leaning to either side
Walk for the prescribed distance or time, then set the weights down

exercises.name: Back Extension
exercises.description: A lower back exercise performed on a hyperextension bench by hinging at the hips and extending the torso back to a straight position.
instructions: Position yourself on a hyperextension bench with your hips on the pad and feet secured
Cross your arms over your chest or place your hands behind your head
Lower your torso toward the floor by hinging at the hips
Lower until your torso is roughly perpendicular to the floor
Raise your torso back up until your body forms a straight line
Avoid hyperextending beyond a neutral spine position

exercises.name: Reverse Hyperextension
exercises.description: A lower back and glute exercise where the legs are raised behind the body while the torso remains stationary, decompressing the spine.
instructions: Lie face down on a flat bench or reverse hyper machine with your hips at the edge
Grip the sides of the bench or the handles for stability
Let your legs hang straight down toward the floor
Raise your legs by squeezing your glutes and lower back until they are in line with your torso
Lower your legs back down under control

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.description: An isometric core exercise where you hold a push-up-like position on your forearms, building deep core stability and endurance.
instructions: Start on the floor on your forearms and toes
Keep your elbows directly under your shoulders
Maintain a straight line from your head to your heels
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes
Hold the position for the prescribed duration without letting your hips sag or pike up

exercises.name: Cable Crunches
exercises.description: A weighted abdominal exercise performed kneeling at a cable station, using the cable resistance to intensify the crunch motion.
instructions: Kneel in front of a cable machine with the pulley set to the highest position
Grip the rope attachment and hold it beside your head
Curl your torso downward by contracting your abs, bringing your elbows toward your knees
Squeeze your abs at the bottom of the movement
Slowly return to the upright kneeling position

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.description: An advanced abdominal exercise performed by raising the legs while hanging from a pull-up bar, targeting the lower abs.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip and arms fully extended
Keep your legs straight or slightly bent
Raise your legs by flexing at the hips until they are parallel to the floor or higher
Squeeze your abs at the top of the movement
Lower your legs back to the starting position under control
Avoid swinging or using momentum

exercises.name: Russian Twist
exercises.description: A rotational core exercise performed seated on the floor, twisting the torso side to side to target the obliques and deep core muscles.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet slightly elevated
Lean your torso back to about a 45-degree angle
Hold a dumbbell or medicine ball with both hands in front of your chest
Rotate your torso to one side, bringing the weight beside your hip
Rotate to the opposite side in a controlled manner
Continue alternating sides for the prescribed repetitions

exercises.name: Neck Curl
exercises.description: An isolation exercise for the front of the neck performed lying face up, strengthening the neck flexors for injury prevention.
instructions: Lie face up on a flat bench with your head hanging off the edge
Place a light weight plate on your forehead, holding it with both hands
Let your head drop back slightly below the level of the bench
Curl your head forward by tucking your chin toward your chest
Lower your head back to the starting position under control

exercises.name: Neck Extension
exercises.description: An isolation exercise for the rear of the neck performed lying face down, strengthening the neck extensors and upper traps.
instructions: Lie face down on a flat bench with your head hanging off the edge
Place a light weight plate on the back of your head, holding it with both hands
Let your head drop forward slightly below the level of the bench
Extend your head upward by contracting the muscles on the back of your neck
Lower your head back to the starting position under control
